headline: Which Airline Stock Has Dominated in 2026: Delta, United, American, or JetBlue?
summary: Airline stocks are trading lower across the board this Wednesday afternoon, but the more revealing story for investors is how dramatically the group has diverged in 2026. Through mid-June, Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) stock leads the four major U.S. carrier stocks by a wide margin, while American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL) stock sits at the bottom of ... Which Airline Stock Has Dominated in 2026: Delta, United, American, or JetBlue?

headline: A Look At United Airlines (UAL) Valuation As CEO Targets Rival Assets Over Full Mergers
summary: United Airlines Holdings (UAL) stock is back in focus after CEO Scott Kirby highlighted a plan to pursue airport slots, gates and other assets from financially stressed rivals instead of large merger deals. See our latest analysis for United Airlines Holdings. The recent 1-day share price return of 4.09% and 90-day share price return of 20.90% suggest momentum has picked up again, even though the year-to-date share price return is down 2.99%. headline: United Unveils New Menu Items from World's Leading Chefs
summary: United serves up a new era of inflight dining on August 1 with the debut of new chef-curated meals from its collaboration with Chef's Table, the brand behind the Emmy award-winning Netflix series.

headline: United Airlines Targets Distressed Rival Assets To Refine Premium Network Growth
summary: United Airlines Holdings CEO Scott Kirby signaled interest in buying airport slots, gates, and other key assets from financially stressed competitors, while ruling out large mergers. The comments highlight a focus on targeted asset deals during a period of higher fuel costs and potential pressure on weaker airlines. For investors tracking NasdaqGS:UAL, this potential shift in how United grows comes with the stock at $109.63 and a 1 year return of 31.8%. Over 3 years, the stock return is...
